Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of SUR1 abolishes both aliphatic and aromatic glucosinolate accumulation and causes high-auxin phenotype through diversion of IAOx toward IAA
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SUR1 encodes the C-S lyase step in glucosinolate core biosynthesis. 2. Knockout mutants show complete absence of both aliphatic and aromatic glucosinolates. 3. sur1 mutants display high-auxin phenotypes, indicating metabolic competition between glucosinolate biosynthesis and IAA production.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Bioinformatics-based gene identification; knockout mutant metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In the review text SUR1 is treated as identical to the glucosinolate-pathway C-S lyase and also named superroot1/afl1/rooty/bls3 in prior literature; classified here as Common Gene because the extraction schema has no dedicated degradation/auxiliary enzyme category beyond Pathway/Regulation/Common and the paper emphasizes its metabolic branchpoint role.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related specialized metabolites; branchpoint competition with IAA biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes glucosylation to desulfoglucosinolate; knockout significantly decreases but does not abolish glucosinolate accumulation and gives a mild high-auxin phenotype
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT74B1 glucosylates thiohydroximic acids to form desulfoglucosinolates. 2. Knockout mutants retain partial glucosinolate accumulation, indicating redundancy by other UGTs. 3. Reduced pathway flux can shift indole metabolism toward auxin-related phenotypes.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homology-based identification; biochemical enzyme characterization; knockout analysis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense, flavor, biofumigation, cancer-preventive hydrolysis-product precursor
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified based on homology to a patented Brassica napus ortholog.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directs glucosinolate hydrolysis away from isothiocyanates toward epithionitriles and simple nitriles in the presence of myrosinase
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ESP is a protein factor controlling the direction of glucosinolate breakdown products. 2. Arabidopsis ESP promotes both epithionitrile and simple nitrile formation. 3. It acts with myrosinase but has no known catalytic activity alone.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Alters ecological and nutritional outcomes of glucosinolate breakdown
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Arabidopsis Columbia lacks functional ESP according to the review; ESP affects hydrolysis outcome rather than biosynthetic accumulation.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Isothiocyanates and other glucosinolate hydrolysis products
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hydrolyze glucosinolates after tissue damage to release glucose and unstable aglycones that generate bioactive defense and flavor products
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis contains four functional myrosinase genes. 2. Myrosinases initiate glucosinolate breakdown and require ascorbate as cofactor. 3. Spatial compartmentation of myrosinase helps prevent hydrolysis in intact tissue and enables the mustard oil bomb after damage.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical characterization; X-ray crystallography; promoter-GUS localization studies
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Extracellular/vacuolar-associated myrosin cells; idioblasts and guard cells
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against herbivores and pathogens; flavor and health-related isothiocyanate formation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific Arabidopsis functional myrosinase genes are known in the cited literature but individual gene-specific metabolic distinctions are not detailed in this review excerpt.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Amino acid chain elongation of methionine derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses ω-methylthio-2-oxoalkanoates with acetyl-CoA to form 2-(ω-methylthioalkyl)malates during methionine chain elongation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control locus for methionine-derived glucosinolate chain length
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Determines glucosinolate chain-length polymorphism; functional MAM1 correlates with glucosinolates formed after two elongation cycles
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Fine mapping; missense mutants; heterologous expression in E. coli; enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MAM1 underlies the GS-Elong chain-length polymorphism between Arabidopsis ecotypes. 2. Recombinant MAM1 catalyzes the condensation step of methionine chain elongation. 3. MAM1 primarily supports the first two elongation cycles and controls accumulation of specific aliphatic glucosinolate chain lengths.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for redesigning aliphatic glucosinolate chain-length profile in Brassicaceae crops; may be combined with MAM-L/MAM2 and downstream tailoring genes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mapped also as a key locus in Brassica napus-related studies; Arabidopsis Col-0 knockout is deficient in C4 aliphatic glucosinolates.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Amino acid chain elongation of methionine derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream; chain-elongation condensing step for later cycles
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Supports additional methionine chain-elongation condensation reactions required for long-chain aliphatic glucosinolate formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key determinant for long-chain aliphatic glucosinolate formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout abolishes long-chain methionine-derived glucosinolates; transformation with functional gene restores them
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MAM-L is required for long-chain methionine-derived glucosinolate biosynthesis. 2. Loss of MAM-L eliminates long-chain products. 3. Complementation restores glucosinolate accumulation, confirming causal function.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target to remove or restore long-chain aliphatic glucosinolates in crop breeding or engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also referred to in metabolic engineering section as MAML; Col-0 knockout deficient in C6-C8 aliphatic glucosinolates.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Amino acid chain elongation of methionine derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Associated with methionine chain elongation leading primarily to glucosinolates having undergone one round of elongation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional MAM2 is correlated with accumulation of glucosinolates formed after one elongation cycle
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MAM2 is an alternative functional allele/gene at the GS-Elong locus in many ecotypes. 2. Presence of functional MAM2 correlates with one-cycle chain-elongated products. 3. MAM1 and MAM2 distribution underlies natural variation in aliphatic glucosinolate profile.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Most ecotypes possess either functional MAM1 or MAM2, but not both.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: committed step upstream entry to indole branch
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-entry step for indole glucosinolate formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with CYP79B3 is essential for indole glucosinolate biosynthesis; overexpression increases indole glucosinolates and contributes to IAA/camalexin branch flux
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast cDNA screen; heterologous expression; double knockout; overexpression; GUS reporter analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP79B2 converts tryptophan to IAOx. 2. CYP79B2 and CYP79B3 double knockout plants lack indole glucosinolates. 3. CYP79B2 also contributes to IAA and camalexin-related branch metabolism from IAOx.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Induced during camalexin production; responds to environmental cues less strongly than CYP79B3 to methyl jasmonate
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Overexpression elevates indole glucosinolates; useful for engineering defense and health-related glucosinolate profiles but may perturb auxin metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: 35S::CYP79B2 gives about fivefold increase in indole glucosinolates; root meristem expression supports local IAA synthesis role.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: committed step upstream entry to indole branch
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-entry step for indole glucosinolate formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts redundantly with CYP79B2 to supply IAOx; double knockout eliminates indole glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression; double knockout; reporter analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP79B3 is a tryptophan N-hydroxylase/aldoxime-forming P450 for indole glucosinolate biosynthesis. 2. Functional redundancy with CYP79B2 is sufficient to mask single-gene loss. 3. CYP79B3 is particularly induced by methyl jasmonate.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Primarily induced by methyl jasmonate and wounding-associated signals
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential inducible engineering target for defense-related indole glucosinolate enhancement.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Conversion of chain-elongated methionine derivatives to aldoximes
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: entry step to aliphatic core-structure formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts chain-elongated methionine homologs from mono- to hexahomomethionine into corresponding aldoximes
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; overexpression suggests chain elongation rather than CYP79F1 may be limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout eliminates short-chain aliphatic glucosinolates and causes severe morphological defects; overexpression increases aliphatic glucosinolates modestly
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (bushy, supershoot); biochemical characterization; overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP79F1 metabolizes chain-elongated methionines spanning short- and long-chain homologs. 2. Knockout mutants lack short-chain aliphatic glucosinolates and show altered morphology. 3. Overexpression only modestly increases aliphatic glucosinolates, indicating upstream limitation in chain elongation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Severe morphological phenotype with many axillary shoots when knocked out; altered hormone balance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for modulating aliphatic glucosinolate content, but manipulation can alter morphology and hormone balance.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Conversion of long-chain methionine derivatives to aldoximes
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: entry step to aliphatic core-structure formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts long-chain penta- and hexahomomethionines into corresponding aldoximes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout substantially reduces long-chain aliphatic glucosinolates while leaving short-chain glucosinolates largely unaffected
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP79F2 has narrower substrate specificity than CYP79F1. 2. It preferentially supports long-chain aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthesis. 3. Genetic analysis alone underestimated overlap and specificity, highlighting need for biochemistry.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts phenylalanine to the corresponding aldoxime for aromatic glucosinolate formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports biosynthesis of phenylalanine-derived glucosinolates; overexpression increases benzyl glucosinolate
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP79A2 is one of the five characterized Arabidopsis CYP79s for major glucosinolate classes. 2. It generates aldoxime precursor from phenylalanine. 3. 35S::CYP79A2 plants accumulate increased benzyl glucosinolate.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to elevate benzyl glucosinolate in engineering approaches.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Aldoxime to activated oxidized intermediate/thiohydroximate branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes aldoximes, especially indole and other aromatic aldoximes, to a reactive activated intermediate that is trapped by sulfur donor to form S-alkyl thiohydroximates
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Reactive oxidized aldoxime intermediate leading to S-alkyl thiohydroximates
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key metabolic branchpoint controlling diversion of IAOx away from IAA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss causes high-auxin phenotype and disrupts indole glucosinolate flux; preferentially metabolizes aromatic aldoximes with high affinity for IAOx
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP83B1 is the principal aromatic/indole aldoxime-metabolizing enzyme in Arabidopsis glucosinolate biosynthesis. 2. Its high affinity for IAOx explains nonredundancy with CYP83A1 under physiological conditions. 3. Knockout mutants accumulate auxin-related phenotypes due to altered branch flux.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also known as SUR2/superroot2/runt1/atr4 in mutant literature.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Aldoxime to activated oxidized intermediate/thiohydroximate branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes primarily aliphatic aldoximes in glucosinolate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Aliphatic aldoximes; also aromatic aldoximes at lower affinity
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Reactive oxidized aldoxime intermediate leading to S-alkyl thiohydroximates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout reduces aliphatic glucosinolates and unexpectedly increases indole glucosinolates; links glucosinolate and phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP83A1 preferentially metabolizes aliphatic aldoximes. 2. cyp83A1 mutants have lower aliphatic glucosinolates and altered phenylpropanoid levels. 3. Crosstalk with indole glucosinolate pathway may occur through metabolic stress and CYP79B2/B3 upregulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified initially from altered phenylpropanoid phenotype; reduced sinapoylmalate reported.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers sulfate from PAPS to desulfoglucosinolates to form glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes final sulfation step with preference for tryptophan- and phenylalanine-derived desulfoglucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtST5a encodes the terminal sulfotransferase step of core glucosinolate biosynthesis. 2. It preferentially sulfates indole and aromatic desulfoglucosinolates. 3. Identification of ST5 enzymes nearly completed the core pathway gene set.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers sulfate from PAPS to long-chain aliphatic desulfoglucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions in the last core step with preference for long-chain aliphatic desulfoglucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtST5b is a close homolog of AtST5a. 2. It preferentially sulfates long-chain aliphatic desulfoglucosinolates. 3. Sulfotransferase specialization contributes to class-specific glucosinolate formation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers sulfate from PAPS to long-chain aliphatic desulfoglucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions in the final biosynthetic step with preference for long-chain aliphatic desulfoglucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtST5c is another terminal sulfotransferase in glucosinolate core biosynthesis. 2. It prefers long-chain aliphatic substrates. 3. Multiple ST5 paralogs together complete glucosinolate sulfation chemistry.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts methylsulfinylalkyl glucosinolates to alkenyl glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Determines ecotype-specific accumulation of alkenyl glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL mapping; heterologous expression in E. coli; promoter/ORF comparison across ecotypes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOP2 is a functionally characterized side-chain modifying dioxygenase. 2. Expression of functional AOP2 is associated with ecotypes accumulating alkenyl glucosinolates. 3. Natural promoter and coding-sequence variation underlies chemotypic diversity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Powerful target to shift health- and defense-relevant side-chain composition in Brassicaceae crops.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts methylsulfinylalkyl glucosinolates to hydroxyalkyl glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Determines ecotype-specific accumulation of hydroxyalkyl glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOP3 is a side-chain modifying dioxygenase of methionine-derived glucosinolates. 2. It converts methylsulfinylalkyl substrates to hydroxyalkyl glucosinolates. 3. Differential promoter activity contributes to natural glucosinolate variation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts tyrosine to p-hydroxyphenylacetaldoxime, which endogenous Arabidopsis glucosinolate enzymes further convert to p-hydroxybenzyl glucosinolate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Heterologous expression produces high levels of a nonnative glucosinolate in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression under 35S promoter; radiolabel feeding
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Sorghum CYP79A1 can be functionally inserted into Arabidopsis glucosinolate metabolism. 2. Its product is accepted by endogenous downstream enzymes. 3. This demonstrates plasticity of the postaldoxime pathway for generating novel glucosinolates.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Enables creation of novel glucosinolate chemotypes in heterologous hosts.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts valine and isoleucine to aldoxime precursors that endogenous Arabidopsis enzymes convert into isopropyl and 1-methylpropyl glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces novel glucosinolates not normally present or only trace-present in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cassava CYP79D2 enables production of valine- and isoleucine-derived glucosinolates in Arabidopsis. 2. This shows broad side-chain permissiveness of downstream glucosinolate biosynthetic enzymes. 3. Heterologous CYP79s are practical tools for metabolic diversification.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for generating novel glucosinolate profiles for defense or nutritional applications.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator that coordinately upregulates indole glucosinolate biosynthetic genes and increases indole glucosinolate accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conditionally decisive positive regulator for indole glucosinolate pathway output
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates expression of CYP79B2, CYP79B3, CYP83B1, and ASA1
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Indole glucosinolate biosynthesis; coordinated branch control around tryptophan/IAOx metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gain-of-function mutant atr1D; loss-of-function mutant; transcript analysis; metabolite profiling; overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ATR1/AtMYB34 is a positive regulator of indole glucosinolate biosynthesis. 2. atr1D elevates pathway-gene expression and increases indole glucosinolates, whereas loss of function reduces them. 3. ATR1 provides a handle for coordinated regulation of multiple biosynthetic enzymes.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: 35S::ATR1 overexpression is less effective than atr1D for metabolite increase, implying ectopic expression outside proper cells may be suboptimal
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be used to coordinately increase indole glucosinolates, but spatially appropriate expression is important for strong effect.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DF119L QTL likely corresponds to ATR1 according to review discussion.
